wizard.connectHint: You'll need your Jira URL and an API token from Atlassian.
wizard.measureHint: Tell us which statuses mark the start and end of active work.
wizard.measureStartQ: Which status means work has started?
wizard.measureModeFullHint: First entry into start → last entry into done. Captures re-opened tickets.
wizard.measureModeFirstHint: First entry into start → first entry into done. Ignores later rework.
wizard.pickConnHint: A connection stores your credentials so Cylenivo can fetch ticket data. Choose an existing one or add a new one.
wizard.statusesHint: Drag to reorder, remove any you don't need. Only these will appear in the next step.
wizard.statusesEmpty: No statuses left — go back and re-fetch.

forecast.title: Forecast
forecast.subtitle: Probabilistic delivery estimates based on your historical throughput.
forecast.lowDataWarning: Only {{count}} completed tickets in history — results may be unreliable. More data improves accuracy.
forecast.basis: Based on {{weeks}} weeks of history · {{weeksWithData}} weeks with completions · {{completed}} tickets total
forecast.emptyHint: Choose between How many? (capacity in N weeks) or When? (weeks needed for N tickets), enter a number and hit Simulate.

onboarding.title: Welcome to Cylenivo
onboarding.subtitle: Understand your team's delivery speed — cycle time, lead time and throughput, all from your Jira data.
onboarding.demosReady: Three demo projects are ready to explore
onboarding.improvingDesc: Simple 4-status workflow. Metrics improve steadily over time — a clean baseline for getting familiar with the tool.
onboarding.complexDesc: 8-status workflow with rework cycles. A realistic scenario showing complexity and variability.
onboarding.realworldDesc: 434 tickets across Jan–Dec 2025. The team rebuilt their board twice, leaving orphaned statuses, zombie tickets, quick wins, and rework — a stress test for all metrics.
onboarding.ownData: Ready to analyze your own team?
onboarding.removeTip: Remove demo projects anytime via Settings → Data Management

welcome.title: Welcome to Cylenivo
welcome.subtitle: Understand your team's delivery speed. Connect to Jira directly or upload an export file — analyze cycle time, lead time and throughput in minutes.
welcome.featureCycleTimeDesc: How long tickets take from start to done
welcome.featureThroughputDesc: How many tickets ship per week
welcome.cta: Connect to Jira directly or upload an export — takes about 2 minutes.

dashboard.noDatasetHint: Upload a Jira export to start analyzing

percentile.title: Cycle Time Forecast
percentile.basedOn: Based on {{metricName}} of completed tickets. Shows how long future tickets are likely to take.
percentile.p85Explain: P85 means: 85% of tickets completed within this many days.

import.title: Import Data
import.dragDrop: Drag and drop your JSON export here
import.chooseSourceHint: Where does your ticket data come from?
import.fetchFromJiraHint: Select a connection and configure what to fetch.
import.noConnectionsHint: Add a Jira connection in Settings first.
import.completedBetweenHint: — optional, only imports tickets resolved in this window
import.uploadYourExportHint: Drop a JSON file exported from Jira or another tool.
import.configureMetricsHint: Tell the analyzer which statuses mark the start and end of cycle time.
import.leadTimeEndHint: — empty = same as cycle time end

insights.title: AI Insights
insights.subtitle: AI-powered analysis of your flow metrics
insights.noLlmHint: Configure an LLM in Settings → AI to enable analysis and chat.
insights.checkConfig: Check your configuration in Settings → AI
insights.noAnalysisHint: Click "Run Analysis" to generate AI insights
insights.chatEmpty: Ask anything about your flow metrics

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage data sources, configurations, datasets, AI and app preferences
settings.confirmDelete: Delete this configuration? This cannot be undone.
settings.dataSourcesDesc: Manage your Jira connections and import settings. Each data source stores credentials and default parameters for fetching.
settings.noDataSourcesHint: Add a Jira connection to start importing tickets.
settings.configsDesc: Measurement rules — define which statuses mark cycle time start and end. One configuration can be reused for multiple datasets of the same team.
settings.noConfigsHint: A configuration is created automatically when you import your first dataset.
settings.datasetsDesc: Imported ticket snapshots — each dataset uses one configuration to calculate metrics. Import the same project again over time to track trends.
settings.noDatasetsHint: Connect to Jira or upload an export to get started.
settings.connectionsDesc: Stored Jira credentials — saved once, reused for all imports. The API token is never sent to the browser after saving.
settings.connectionAddedHint: Now go to Import to fetch your first dataset.
settings.noConnectionsHint: Add your Jira credentials once — then fetch tickets directly from the Import wizard.
settings.aiDesc: Configure the AI model used for flow analysis and chat. API keys are stored locally and never leave your device.
settings.overviewDesc: Connect a data source, import your tickets and start measuring.
settings.addNewDataDesc: Walk through connecting a data source, importing tickets, and setting up your measurement configuration.
settings.noSetup: No data imported yet. Add new data to get started.
settings.manageDataDesc: View and edit your data sources, configurations, and datasets.
settings.dataManagementDesc: Permanently delete all project data or restore the built-in demo datasets.
settings.deleteEverythingDesc: All configurations, datasets, and tickets will be permanently deleted. Connections and AI settings are kept. This cannot be undone.
settings.completeResetDesc: Deletes all data including connections and AI settings. Resets the app to a clean state — as if installed for the first time.
settings.completeResetConfirmDesc: All data will be permanently deleted: datasets, configurations, connections, and AI settings. The app will reload. This cannot be undone.
settings.completeResetDone: App has been reset. Reload to start fresh.
settings.pluginsDesc: Extend Cylenivo with additional data source connectors.
settings.pluginsComingSoonDesc: Extend Cylenivo with new data sources — Azure DevOps, Linear, GitHub Projects, and more. Plugins run locally as Node.js subprocesses.
settings.languageDesc: Choose the display language for the interface.
settings.languageComingSoon: Additional languages are coming soon. The interface is currently English only.

dataset.editDesc: Change the display name or reassign the measurement configuration.
dataset.name: Name
dataset.nameHint: Human-readable label shown in the sidebar and settings list.
dataset.configHint: Changing the configuration will recalculate all metrics for this dataset.

config.defineMetrics: Define how cycle time and lead time are measured
config.loadFromImportHint: Select an existing dataset to automatically detect all status values.
config.statusesLoaded: ✓ {{count}} statuses loaded — now select cycle time start and end below.
config.name: Name
config.modeFirstFirst: First in / First out — time to first done
config.modeLastLast: Last in / Last out — last active period only

sidebar.deleteConfirmDesc: This will permanently delete "{{name}}" and all its data. This cannot be undone.
sidebar.noIssues: No issues detected. Data looks clean.
sidebar.noDataYetHint: Import your first dataset to start analyzing.
sidebar.neverEnteredImpact: These tickets are fully excluded from cycle time, lead time and throughput. They likely used an older workflow or a different process. No impact on accuracy.
sidebar.neverEnteredAction: No action needed unless you want to include these tickets — in that case, check if the cycle start status in your configuration matches the actual workflow.
sidebar.incompleteImpact: These are in-progress or abandoned tickets. They are excluded from cycle time and lead time, but visible in Time in Status up to their last known transition.
sidebar.incompleteAction: Normal for any active team. If the number seems high, check whether the cycle end status in your configuration matches where tickets actually finish.
sidebar.unknownImpact: Time spent in these statuses is invisible in the Time in Status chart: {{statuses}}. The chart totals will not add up to the full cycle time.
sidebar.unknownAction: Add the relevant statuses to your configuration's status order if you want to see time spent there. Statuses you don't care about can be left out.
sidebar.oldDataImpact: Throughput is averaged over the full date range — if old tickets are included, the weekly throughput number will be much lower than your current actual velocity. Cycle time averages may also reflect older team behaviour.
sidebar.oldDataAction: Re-import with the "Completed between" date filter (e.g. last 12–18 months) to get metrics that reflect how your team works today.

flow.title: Analysis
flow.subtitle: Understand where time is spent in your workflow

rework.title: Rework Analysis
rework.noRework: No backward movements detected — clean flow!

board.title: Time Distribution Across Statuses
board.footer: Column width proportional to average time spent. Histogram shows ticket distribution.

timeInStatus.avgSubtitle: Average per status within the cycle window ({{start}} → {{end}}). Only analyzed tickets included.
timeInStatus.howToReadDesc: Shows the average time each ticket spent in a status during its cycle — from entry into {{start}} to last entry into {{end}}.
timeInStatus.howToReadExclude: Only statuses within this window are shown. Tickets that did not complete the cycle are excluded.
timeInStatus.howToReadRework: If {{status}} shows a value > 0, some tickets returned from {{status}} to an earlier status (rework) before completing.

connection.name: Name
connection.defaultSettingsHint: Pre-filled when importing from this connection
connection.errUnauthorized: Email or API token is incorrect. Double-check your Atlassian API token and the email address you use to log into Jira.
connection.errForbidden: Your API token doesn't have permission to access this Jira instance. Make sure the token belongs to a user with read access.
connection.errNotFound: Jira URL not found. Make sure it looks like https://yourcompany.atlassian.net.
connection.errUnreachable: Could not reach Jira. Check that the URL is correct and that you have an internet connection.
connection.errTimeout: Connection timed out. Jira took too long to respond — check the URL and try again.

errors.serverCrashed: The background server stopped unexpectedly. Please restart the app.

help.cycleTime: Time from when work starts (cycle start status) to when it's done (cycle end status). Measures how long active work takes.
help.leadTime: Time from ticket creation (or lead time start status) to completion. Includes wait time before work begins — always ≥ cycle time.
help.throughput: Average number of tickets completed per week, based on when tickets reached the cycle time end status.
help.throughputNote: Does not depend on lead time configuration.
help.analyzed: Only tickets with a valid cycle time are analyzed. Tickets that never entered the cycle start status or never completed are excluded.
help.cycleTimeTrend: Each dot is one completed ticket. X-axis: completion date. Y-axis: cycle time in days. The P85 line marks the 85th percentile — 85% of tickets finished faster.
help.leadTimeTrend: Each dot is one completed ticket. X-axis: completion date. Y-axis: lead time in days. The P85 line marks the 85th percentile.
help.weeklyThroughput: Completed tickets per calendar week. The dashed line shows the weekly average. Empty weeks are included to make gaps visible.
help.cfd: Shows how many tickets are in each status on any given day. A widening band means tickets are piling up in that status — a bottleneck. The gap between two bands approximates cycle time at that point.
help.timeRange: Filter all charts to a specific date range. Drag the handles to narrow the window — only tickets completed within the selected range will be included.
help.cycleTimeByType: Compares cycle time across ticket types. Median shows the typical value, P85 shows the upper bound for 85% of tickets.
help.rework: Rework means a ticket moved backward in the workflow — e.g. from Review back to Development. More rework usually means longer cycle times.
help.reworkPaths: Most frequent backward status transitions across all analyzed tickets.
help.boardVisualization: Each column is one status. Width shows average time spent — wider columns are bottlenecks. Histogram shows how tickets are distributed within each status.
help.boardColors: Teal = cycle time window. Violet = lead time only. Gray = outside both windows.
help.configContext: Shows your configured workflow. Teal = cycle time statuses. Violet = lead time only. Gray = outside both windows.
help.configMode: Determines how entry/exit times are picked when a ticket visits a status multiple times.
help.modeFirstLast: First entry into start status → last entry into end status. Captures full time including rework loops.
help.modeFirstFirst: First entry into start → first entry into end. Measures time to first completion, ignoring later rework.
help.modeLastLast: Last entry into start → last entry into end. Only the most recent active period.
help.ticketColors: Green = faster than P50 (median). Amber = between P50 and P85. Red = slower than P85.
help.analyzedOnly: When active, only shows tickets that have a valid cycle time — they entered the start status and reached the end status.
help.ticketTimeline: Shows how this ticket moved through statuses over time. Hover to see duration per status. Rose-colored segments indicate backward movements (rework).
help.statusOrder: The workflow statuses in order, from first to last. Drag to reorder. Cycle time and lead time are measured between statuses you pick from this list.
help.projectKey: The project identifier in your issue tracker (e.g. TN, PROJ). In Jira, it's the prefix before the ticket number.
help.issueTypes: Which ticket types to import. Deselect types you don't want in your analysis (e.g. Epics if you only track stories).
help.maxTickets: Limits the number of tickets fetched. Start small (50–100) for a quick test, increase for full analysis.
help.cycleTimeConfig: Define which statuses mark the start and end of active work. Cycle time is measured between these two statuses.
help.leadTimeConfig: Lead time starts at ticket creation (or a specific status) and ends when the ticket is done. It includes cycle time plus any wait time before work starts.
help.llmProvider: Ollama runs locally on your machine (free, private). OpenAI uses the cloud API (requires API key). OpenAI compatible works with any API that follows the OpenAI format.
help.llmBaseUrl: The URL where the AI service is running. For Ollama, this is usually http://localhost:11434.
help.llmSystemPrompt: Instructions that shape how the AI analyzes your data. The default prompt is optimized for flow metrics — only change this if you know what you're doing.
help.perTicketBreakdown: Time per status for each of the last 30 completed tickets, sorted by completion date. Shows how individual tickets flowed through your process.
help.connName: A friendly label for this connection — only shown inside Cylenivo.
help.connBaseUrl: The root URL of your Jira instance, e.g. https://yourcompany.atlassian.net. Found in your browser's address bar when you open Jira.
help.connEmail: The email address you use to log into Jira.
help.connApiToken: A personal API token generated in your Atlassian account — not your login password. Cylenivo uses it to read tickets on your behalf.

about.tagline: Flow metrics for software teams — local, private, no accounts.
